title: hyssopus officinalis subsp. montanus
intro: Hyssopus officinalis subsp. montanus, also known as mountain hyssop, is a subspecies of the perennial herb Hyssopus officinalis, native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of mountain hyssop include relief from respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce stress. Mountain hyssop can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties, or used topically as a skin salve or oil to treat skin irritations and wounds.
